results.title: Volkswagen Group to slash lineup by up to 50 per cent by end of decade
results.description: Volkswagen Group is planning to reduce its global portfolio by up to 50 per cent by 2030. The brand will also streamline the number of vehicle platforms and electronic architectures used across its brands.

results.title: Rupee falls 62 paise to close at 96.30 against US dollar
results.description: The rupee depreciated 62 paise to close at 96.30 (provisional) against the US dollar on Tuesday, amid a surge in crude oil prices and renewed geopolitical concerns.

results.title: India’s Strong Message To Iran After Sailor’s Death In Hormuz Ship Strike
results.description: New Delhi: India has summoned Iran’s deputy chief of mission in New Delhi after an Indian crew member was killed while eight others, including six Indians, sustained injuries in an Iranian strike on the United Arab Emirates’ two shipping tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. results.title: Disrupting Wealth Architecture: How Don Kilam’s $35.8M Enterprise is Decentralizing Asset Protection
results.description: LAS VEGAS, NV — The traditional financial system is built on consumer-level exposure, but a growing movement of sovereign estate administration is shifting the landscape. Leading private asset strategist Don Kilam has announced the operational expansion of his multi-million-dollar enterprise, Kilam International. The move marks a key scaling phase... results.title: BREAKING: House of Reps Withdraws Own State Police Bill
results.description: The House of Representatives has withdrawn its own proposed constitutional amendment seeking to establish state police across Nigeria.The development comes as lawmakers moved ahead with consideration of a separate state police bill forwarded by the Executive arm of government.During plenary on Tuesday, the House passed the Executive-sponsored bill through its first and second readings. results.title: Sarawak govt to review FWTA fees to ease burden on businesses
results.description: KUCHING (July 14): The Sarawak government will study possible revisions to the fee mechanism under the Foreign Workers Transformation Approach (FWTA) to ease the financial burden on the business community. Premier Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg gave the assurance during a courtesy call by the Associated Chinese Chambers of Commerce and Industry [...] The post Sarawak govt to review FWTA fees to ease burden on businesses appeared first on Borneo Post Online .
